The public robots.txt allowed the verified public source paths and listed a crawl delay and sitemap.

artifacts.summary: Official discovery found a public University of Oulu AI-in-education guideline, a public responsible-research page that confirms separate GenAI research guidance for researchers, and an official library guide for AI tools in information searching. Patio sources were login-gated and repository/news sources were not used for policy claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of Oulu has drawn up common policies on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in education. The guidelines describe what kind of use of AI is allowed or prohibited at the University of Oulu and how it can be used responsibly to support studies.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The University of Oulu has common AI-in-education guidelines describing allowed, prohibited, and responsible uses.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For a given assignment or course, the teacher may, at their discretion, whether the use of AI is: 1) required (must 2 be used, must be reported, affects assessment); 2) prohibited (not to be used); 3) restricted (can be used, must be reported); or 4) allowed (can be used, need not be reported).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Teachers may set assignment or course AI use as required, prohibited, restricted, or allowed.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The student is always personally responsible for the output they submit, and output produced by AI may not be presented as one's own or as a source. AI can provide study support but it is not a substitute for one’s own learning, thought, and practice.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students remain responsible for submitted output and may not present AI output as their own or as a source.

artifacts.notes: Claim is scoped to the public education guideline and does not treat the staged output as canonical.
Claim mirrors the traffic-light/course-discretion language without converting it into a blanket rule for all assignments.
Claim is limited to responsibility and non-presentation of AI output as one's own or as a source.
Claim preserves the conditional 'when AI use is permitted' scope and does not identify approved tools beyond the source language.
Claim states only that the public page says guidelines exist; it does not summarize the login-gated full guidance.
Claim is explicitly scoped to the library guide and student information-retrieval workflow.

artifacts.title: University of Oulu staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: The staged bundle identifies public University of Oulu AI-in-education guidance, a public responsible-research page confirming GenAI research guidance for researchers, and an official library guide for AI tools in information searching. Patio guidance pages and a DeepSeek advisory were login-gated and were recorded as inaccessible.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The full Patio and SharePoint research guidance could not be used because it required login; only the public Responsible research page's statement that such guidance exists was used.
Library guide evidence is scoped to information searching and should not be treated as a university-wide binding AI policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
